topic: Feynman Lectures on Physics Volume I Part V (Chapters 33-40): Polarization, Relativistic Radiation, Vision, Quantum Behavior, and Kinetic Theory. Topics: Polarization (linear, circular, Brewster's angle, birefringence), Relativistic effects in radiation (synchrotron radiation, Cherenkov, Doppler), Color vision (physiology of cones, trichromatic theory, color mixing), Mechanisms of seeing (rod/cone cells, neural processing, visual cortex), Quantum behavior (wave-particle duality, double-slit with electrons, probability amplitudes), Wave-particle viewpoints (complementarity, Heisenberg uncertainty, measurement problem), Kinetic theory of gases (Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution, equipartition, mean free path), Principles of statistical mechanics (ensembles, entropy, partition function). Deep research into Feynman's pioneering pedagogy bridging classical and quantum physics at Caltech 1963.

I notice your message was empty. How can I help you today? Feel free to share any coding task, code review, debugging issue, or project work you'd like me to assist with.

**Session-Ready Tips:**
- **Clarity improvement**: Please provide a specific task, goal, or question with relevant file paths so I can work efficiently.
- **Expected output form**: Let me know whether you want analysis, code changes, PR creation, research, or something else.
- **Feedback loop**: After each round I will summarize key actions and ask any follow-up questions to stay aligned.
